title: Electrostatic dust removal apparatus and electrode unit thereof
abstract: An electrostatic dust removal apparatus and an electrode unit thereof are disclosed. The electrostatic dust removal apparatus includes a frame, and a plurality of electrode units. The plurality of electrode units include at least one high-potential unit and at least one low-potential unit. The plurality of electrode units are provided on the frame and are spaced apart from each other. The at least one high-potential unit and the at least one low-potential unit are arranged alternately, and an air channel for removing dust is formed between the electrode units adjacent to each other. Each of the plurality of electrode units includes a conducting part and an insulating part, the conducting part is a conductive plastic part and includes an electrical field generator and a conducting end, and the insulating part covers at least a portion of the electrical field generator.
claims: 1. An electrostatic dust removal apparatus, comprising: a frame; and a plurality of electrode units comprising a plurality of high-potential units and a plurality of low-potential units, the plurality of electrode units being provided on the frame and being spaced apart from each other, the plurality of high-potential units and the plurality of low-potential units being arranged alternately, and an air channel for removing dust being formed between the electrode units adjacent to each other; wherein each of the plurality of electrode units comprises a conducting part and an insulating part, the conducting part is a conductive plastic part and comprises an electrical field generator and a conducting end, and the insulating part covers at least a portion of the electrical field generator; and the electrostatic dust removal apparatus further comprises: a high-potential conductor disposed on one side of the frame in an extending direction of the electrode unit and connected to the conducting end of each of the plurality of high-potential units; and a low-potential conductor disposed on another side of the frame that is opposite to the one side of the frame and connected to the conducting end of each of the plurality of low-potential units, and both of the high-potential conductor and the low-potential conductor extending along a direction along which the plurality of high-potential units and the plurality of low-potential units are arranged alternately.
